top_stories.title: Windows Netlogon RCE exploited, domain controllers at risk (CVE-2026-41089)
top_stories.summary: A critical remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2026-41089) in Windows Netlogon is being actively exploited in the wild, according to the Centre for Cybersecurity Belgium. The flaw is a stack-based buffer overflow in the Netlogon service, which handles authentication and security within Windows domain environments, putting domain controllers at risk.

top_stories.title: Microsoft mandates registered authentication for Entra ID password resets
top_stories.summary: Microsoft is implementing stricter security requirements for its Entra ID Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR) portal, requiring users to have registered authentication methods rather than using unregistered contact details. The change affects how users verify their identity during password resets in Microsoft Entra ID, the identity management platform formerly known as Azure Active Directory.

top_stories.title: VU#780781: Casdoor contains multiple authentication bypass and access management vulnerabilities
top_stories.summary: Casdoor versions 2.362.0 and earlier contain multiple vulnerabilities enabling authentication bypass and privilege escalation, tracked under VU#780781. The flaws are specifically related to SAML processing within the open-source identity and access management platform.

top_stories.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
top_stories.summary: Researchers discovered an exploit chain in a popular automation service that combined over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities to achieve potential full compromise. The attack vector highlights risks associated with excessive permissions and non-human identity (NHI) management in complex cloud integrations.

top_stories.title: Microsoft fixes outage affecting MFA setup, MySignIn service
top_stories.summary: Microsoft experienced an outage affecting its MFA setup process and MySign-Ins platform, preventing customers from configuring multi-factor authentication. The incident impacts Microsoft's identity and authentication services used by organizations for managing user sign-in security.

news.title: RSA extends passwordless authentication to Linux environments
news.summary: RSA has expanded its passwordless authentication capabilities to support Linux environments, addressing a gap where Linux users were previously limited to legacy credential-based access. The expansion targets enterprise infrastructure including servers, developer workstations, and critical operational environments across financial services and government sectors.

news.title: Zero trust physical security needs trust decisions at the edge
news.summary: Hikvision's VP of Global Information Security discusses applying zero trust principles to physical security devices such as cameras and door controllers. The interview covers edge-based trust decisions, treating physical devices as IT assets, posture assessment for agentless devices, and lessons from the Mirai botnet.

news.title: Dashlane Discloses Brute-Force Attack, Encrypted Vaults of Fewer Than 20 Users Downloaded
news.summary: Dashlane disclosed that an external threat actor conducted a brute-force attack against user accounts on May 31, 2026, resulting in the download of encrypted vaults belonging to fewer than 20 personal subscription users. The attack specifically targeted two-factor authentication (2FA) mechanisms, raising concerns about the resilience of 2FA implementations in password management solutions.

news.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
news.summary: A security researcher published multiple zero-day exploits, prompting Microsoft to signal potential criminal charges. The dispute centers on vulnerability disclosure practices and Microsoft's legal response to unsanctioned public exploit releases.

news.title: Red Hat npm packages compromised to steal developer credentials
news.summary: Over 30 npm packages under Red Hat's '@redhat-cloud-services' namespace were compromised in a supply-chain attack targeting developer credentials. The attack distributed a new variant of credential-stealing malware called "Miasma," a derivative of the Shai-Hulud malware family.

news.title: Dashlane password manager users locked out by brute force attacks
news.summary: Dashlane password manager users were locked out of their accounts following brute-force attacks that attempted logins from distant geographic locations and unknown devices. The incidents highlight authentication security concerns around password manager platforms and account lockout mechanisms.

news.title: OpenAI requires stronger authentication for users of its most powerful AI models
news.summary: OpenAI is mandating passkey-based authentication for members of its Trusted Access for Cyber (TAC) program starting June 1, 2026, with Yubico playing a significant role in securing this requirement. TAC members with access to OpenAI's most powerful AI models are required to use hardware security keys or passkeys as part of the new authentication policy.

news.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
news.summary: A supply chain attack campaign called Miasma has compromised Red Hat npm packages (@redhat-cloud-services) to steal credentials and secrets from developer machines. The attack uses install-time execution, credential harvesting, CI/CD environment targeting, encrypted exfiltration, and deploys a self-propagating worm.

news.title: OpenAI Codex Authentication Tokens Stolen in codexui-android npm Supply Chain Attack
news.summary: A malicious npm package named "codexui-android" has been discovered stealing OpenAI Codex authentication tokens through a supply chain attack, with over 29,000 weekly downloads recorded. The package was advertised as a legitimate remote web UI for OpenAI Codex on GitHub and npm while covertly exfiltrating authentication credentials.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords, successfully defacing high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official's account with pro-Iranian content. The attack method involved instructions circulated on Telegram showing how to manipulate the AI chatbot into performing unauthorized account password resets, bypassing normal authentication controls.

news.title: Palo Alto GlobalProtect VPN auth bypass flaw now exploited in attacks
news.summary: Palo Alto Networks has confirmed that threat actors are actively exploiting CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass vulnerability in PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN. The flaw allows attackers to bypass authentication controls to breach corporate networks.

news.title: Okta (OKTA) Hits 4-Year High as Earnings Soar
news.summary: Okta's stock price reached a four-year high following strong earnings results. The price increase was driven by investor reaction to the company's financial performance.

news.title: Adding Optional Claims Using Token Configuration in Microsoft Entra ID
news.summary: The article explains how to use Optional Claims via Token Configuration in Microsoft Entra ID to add user attributes such as first and last name to tokens. This feature allows developers to customize token content without requiring complex claim mapping policies.

news.title: Okta writes its own license to kill rogue AI agents
news.summary: Okta is developing a proprietary licensing framework designed to allow customers to shut down rogue or unauthorized AI agents. CEO Todd McKinnon indicated that enterprise customers such as ServiceNow are requesting the ability to revoke or disable AI agent access as a control mechanism.

news.title: Software stocks wrap up best month since 2001 as talk of 'SaaSpocalypse' subsides
news.summary: Software stocks, including Okta, recorded their best monthly performance since 2001, with Okta seeing a record stock price increase. The gains were driven by investor confidence in AI software strategies rather than any specific IAM product or security development.

news.title: Okta Stock Soars 18% on Strong Q1 Earnings Beat and AI Identity Security Momentum
news.summary: Okta's stock surged 18% following a strong Q1 earnings beat, driven by increased demand for AI-powered identity security solutions. The results signal continued revenue growth for the IAM vendor and reinforce its market position in identity security.

news.title: SailPoint、Claude Compliance APIとの統合を発表、AIプラットフォーム向けにエンタープライズ グレードのアイデンティティ セキュリティを提供
news.summary: SailPoint announced an integration with Anthropic's Claude Compliance API to deliver enterprise-grade identity security for AI platforms. This press release is a Japanese-language translation of the original announcement made on May 21, 2026, in the United States.

news.title: What did Okta report about agentic AI demand? #tech
news.summary: Okta reported Q1 revenue of $765M, up 11% year over year, beating analyst expectations of $752M. The company cited agentic AI as a driver of increased demand for identity tools.

news.title: Threat Actors Exploit Critical FortiClient EMS Flaw to Deploy Credential Stealer
news.summary: Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in Fortinet's FortiClient Endpoint Management Server (EMS) to deploy a credential-stealing malware called EKZ Infostealer. The campaign abuses trusted endpoint management infrastructure to distribute the malware across managed endpoints, with Arctic Wolf reporting on the details of the attack chain.

news.title: Microsoft Slams Public Zero-Day Disclosures Amid GitHub Researcher Account Removal
news.summary: Microsoft publicly advocated for Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure (CVD) after removing the GitHub account of a researcher known as Chaotic Eclipse who disclosed multiple zero-days publicly without prior vendor notification. The incident touches on zero-trust and vulnerability management practices relevant to IAM professionals, though the core dispute is about disclosure policy rather than IAM-specific vulnerabilities.

news.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Claude Security Plugin, Azure Priv-Esc, Kali365 MFA Bypass, FIFA Scams +15 More
news.summary: A threat intelligence bulletin covers multiple security topics including an Azure privilege escalation vulnerability, an MFA bypass technique called Kali365, and a Claude security plugin. The roundup also references various other threats including social engineering campaigns and exposed infrastructure issues.

news.title: Okta tops estimates and raises outlook as SentinelOne slides on weak guidance
news.summary: Okta reported quarterly earnings that beat analyst estimates, sending shares up more than 6% in after-hours trading. The company also raised its forward outlook alongside the earnings report.

news.title: Transforming our approach to sensitivity labels at Microsoft with Microsoft Entra - Inside Track Blog
news.summary: Microsoft describes how it extended sensitivity labels to Microsoft Entra security groups using labels policy to control data sharing. The implementation focuses on preventing oversharing while enabling secure self-service access management within its own internal environment.

news.title: Okta tops first-quarter results on agentic AI demand
news.summary: Okta reported first-quarter financial results that exceeded expectations, driven by demand for agentic AI capabilities. CEO Todd McKinnon indicated the company is increasing resource allocation toward agentic AI tools as part of its long-term strategy.

news.title: Orchid Security targets AI agent sprawl with new identity governance tools
news.summary: Orchid Security has extended its Identity Control Plane platform with new tools specifically designed to govern AI agents, addressing gaps in existing IAM models. The company argues that current IAM frameworks cannot adequately handle AI agents that inherit authority from both human users and machine identities.

news.title: Gartner® Names Keeper Security the Second Fastest-Growing Company in the Market Share Analysis: Security Software, Worldwide, 2025
news.summary: Gartner named Keeper Security the second fastest-growing company in its Market Share Analysis: Security Software, Worldwide, 2025 report. The recognition highlights Keeper's PAM platform, KeeperPAM, along with KeeperAI and Keeper Forcefield products.

news.title: Microsoft’s new cloud PCs place AI agents under enterprise controls
news.summary: Microsoft announced Windows 365 for Agents, a cloud PC platform designed to run AI agents within enterprise-controlled secure environments. The platform allows organizations to manage agentic workloads with enterprise controls, including governance over how agents interact with applications, browsers, files, and enterprise systems.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credential leak. Lawmakers from both chambers of Congress are demanding answers from CISA, which is still working to contain the breach and invalidate the exposed credentials.

news.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
news.summary: New Omdia research examines how AI agent identities are creating distinct budget dynamics compared to traditional IAM projects. The research highlights that enterprises must manage, secure, and govern the growing number of AI agent identities proliferating across organizations.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked repository contained files describing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes alongside the privileged access credentials.
